text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer, especially when working in a team. The main goal is to make your code readable and easy to understand. Good naming conventions are key: use clear, descriptive names for variables, functions, and classes so that anyone can grasp their purpose immediately. A well-named function like `calculateTotalPrice()` explains itself without needing comments. Keep your functions short and focused on a single task. This improves maintainability because you can update or fix one small part without affecting the rest of the program. Ultimately, clean code saves time, reduces bugs, and makes collaboration much smoother.
